body: Hey, so Connor's coach pulled me aside after practice today and said he's ready for travel hockey this year. The league registration is $1,800 for the season, plus gear upgrades probably another $400. I think it's a great opportunity for him but wanted to talk before I said yes to anything.
Wow, that's a big jump. Does the $1,800 include ice time and tournaments or are those on top? I'm not saying no, I just want to understand the full number before we commit.

Ice time is included but tournaments are extra — coach estimated maybe 6 tournaments at roughly $150 each for travel and fees, so call it another $900. All in we're probably looking at $3,100 between the two of us. I was thinking we'd split it 50/50.

Okay I ran the numbers. $1,550 is doable for me but it's tight with the fall. I'd feel better if we could cap the tournament travel at 4 this season and see how it goes. I don't want to overcommit him if he burns out, either.

That's actually a fair point — I asked Connor and he said he's nervous about the time commitment too. What if we sign up for the league and cover the 4 tournaments you mentioned, and then revisit after winter break if he wants to add more? I'd rather he enjoy it than feel pressured.
Yes, that works for me. Split the league fee and gear 50/50, cap at 4 tournaments this season and split those too, revisit in January. I'll tell him tonight — he's going to be excited.
Perfect. I'll email the coach and confirm registration. Thanks for talking it through — this is a good call for him.
